skill.name: systems-programming-rust-project
skill.description: Generate Rust project scaffolds with Cargo structure, module organization, testing layout, and development tool configuration for binaries, libraries, workspaces, web APIs, and WebAssembly projects.
skill.summary: Scaffold idiomatic Rust projects with Cargo layouts, modules, tests, documentation, and development tooling.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings were reviewed against SKILL.md. Most static hits are false positives from fenced markdown examples or placeholder text, but the Axum web API scaffold includes permissive CORS and an all-interface bind. Publish with a warning that generated web API defaults need review before production use.

medium_findings.title: Insecure Web API Defaults in Scaffold
medium_findings.description: The Axum template applies permissive CORS and binds the server to 0.0.0.0:3000. This is legitimate scaffold content, but it can expose a generated service broadly if copied into production without changes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact template lines configure permissive cross-origin access and listen on all interfaces. The finding is medium risk because it is example scaffold code, not hidden execution or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External Command Examples Are Documentation
low_findings.description: The static external command hits occur in fenced markdown project examples, Cargo commands, file trees, TOML snippets, Rust snippets, and a Makefile. No executable script or automatic command runner was found in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown fences or sample scaffold content in SKILL.md. Users may still run generated Cargo commands manually, so the residual risk is documented as low.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Placeholder Metadata
low_findings.description: The URL at line 91 is an example repository field in a Cargo.toml template. It does not send data or instruct the assistant to contact an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside package metadata for a generated Rust project. There is no network call, credential use, or data transfer associated with it.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits are caused by ordinary description text and the word command in markdown examples. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm is configured.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found project descriptions and CLI metadata only. No cryptographic API, dependency, or algorithm name is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Match Is Error Text
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance hit points to an InvalidInput error display string in sample Rust error handling. It does not gather host, user, process, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line formats an application error message with caller-provided text. No system discovery command, filesystem enumeration, or environment access appears nearby.

dangerous_patterns.title: Permissive Network Service Template
dangerous_patterns.description: The generated Axum server template combines permissive CORS with a 0.0.0.0 bind address. This pattern should be replaced with explicit origins and deployment-specific bind settings.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Both network settings are visible in the scaffold and can affect generated services. The risk depends on whether users deploy the sample unchanged.

content.value_statement: Rust project setup requires many choices about layout, dependencies, tests, and tooling. This skill generates structured Cargo project plans for common Rust application types.
actual_capabilities: Defines project structures for Rust binaries, libraries, workspaces, web APIs, and WebAssembly projects.
Drafts Cargo.toml dependencies, package metadata, workspace settings, and release profile options.
Creates module layouts for CLI commands, configuration, errors, routes, handlers, services, and tests.
Includes development tooling examples for Makefile tasks, rustfmt settings, and Clippy configuration.
Suggests unit tests, integration tests, benches, examples, README content, and code documentation sections.
limitations: It provides scaffold guidance, not a verified build artifact.
Generated dependency versions may need updates for a specific Rust toolchain.
Web API security settings require review before production deployment.
It does not validate business requirements or generate complete application logic.

use_cases.title: Start a Rust CLI Tool
use_cases.description: Generate a clean binary project layout with CLI parsing, command modules, error handling, tests, and developer commands.

use_cases.title: Plan a Multi-Crate Workspace
use_cases.description: Create a workspace structure that separates core logic, API layers, and command-line entry points across crates.

use_cases.title: Bootstrap a Rust Web API
use_cases.description: Draft an Axum service layout with routes, handlers, models, services, middleware, health checks, and integration tests.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic CLI Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Rust CLI project scaffold for a tool named task-runner. Include the directory layout, Cargo settings, command modules, error handling, tests, and developer commands.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Library Crate
prompt_templates.prompt: Scaffold a Rust library crate for parsing configuration files. Include public modules, error types, examples, documentation sections, integration tests, and minimal dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Workspace Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Rust workspace with crates for core logic, an API layer, and a CLI. Include workspace dependencies, crate boundaries, tests, and release profile settings.

prompt_templates.title: Harden a Web API Scaffold
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an Axum web API scaffold and review the default network, CORS, dependency, testing, logging, and configuration choices for production readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a binary Rust project for a deployment helper CLI.
output_examples.output: A structured project plan with Cargo metadata, source modules, command organization, error handling, tests, examples, and common development tasks.

output_examples.input: Design a Rust workspace for an API service and shared core crate.
output_examples.output: A workspace blueprint with crate responsibilities, shared dependencies, release settings, test placement, and documentation expectations.

output_examples.input: Review a generated Axum scaffold before production use.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering CORS, bind addresses, logging, configuration, dependency versions, error handling, and integration tests.

best_practices: State the project type, deployment target, and expected crate boundaries before asking for scaffolding.
Review generated dependencies, network settings, and security defaults before creating production code.
Ask for tests, documentation, and developer tooling in the same request as the project structure.
anti_patterns: Do not copy web API defaults into production without checking CORS, bind addresses, and authentication needs.
Do not accept broad dependency sets when a smaller library crate would be easier to maintain.
Do not skip tests and examples when scaffolding reusable crates or command-line tools.

faq.question: Does this skill create files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides scaffold plans and examples that an assistant or user can apply in a workspace.

faq.question: Which Rust project types does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers binaries, libraries, workspaces, Axum web APIs, and WebAssembly-oriented project layouts.

faq.question: Can it choose dependencies for my project?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests common dependencies, but versions and feature flags should be reviewed for your toolchain.

faq.question: Is the generated web API scaffold production ready?
faq.answer: It is a starting point. Review CORS, bind addresses, authentication, configuration, logging, and error handling before deployment.

faq.question: Can it help with Rust testing structure?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes unit tests, integration tests, benchmark placement, examples, and common Cargo test commands.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
